source_reviews.notes: The exact Census URL was reopened through a read-only text rendering after the direct request returned HTTP 403; it confirms that 531210 covers establishments acting as agents or brokers in selling, buying, or renting real estate for others.
The PDF reports 41% current AI/GenAI use, 20% daily use, 22% weekly use, 79% e-signature use, and a 2.5% response rate from 1,241 usable responses to a 49,233-member random sample.

The page reports a median member age of 57, 44% of REALTORS over 60, and the repeat/referral figures used as context; the packet correctly discloses that members are not equivalent to brokerage owners.
The page confirms that offers of compensation are prohibited on MLSs, remain negotiable off-MLS, and that buyer agents must enter written agreements before a buyer tours a home.
The page reports a Q2 2025 average of 2.43%, a return toward pre-settlement levels, 2.52% below $500,000, and 2.34% from $500,000 to $999,999.

The filing supports independent-contractor agent economics, the January 2026 completion of Anywhere, and 2025 revenue of $6,961.6 million with commissions and related expense of $5,679.7 million (81.6%). It does not support the executive view's 81.1% label or its description as early-2026 stand-alone brokerage revenue.
The filing states the standard 85/15 split, the annual cap, and 100% agent retention after the cap subject to transaction fees.
The page shows the cited $2.0 million EBITDA transaction at 4.01x and a closed-deal range of 2.5x to 10.8x; the packet discloses the category and anonymity limitations.
The exhibit supports the announced approximately $880 million RE/MAX transaction, 7x fully synergized 2025 EBITDA, and approximately $30 million of expected annual run-rate cost savings.
Rocket's filing states that it completed the Redfin acquisition on July 1, 2025 and describes the integrated mortgage, brokerage, and search platform.
The California DRE page states that a person acting, advertising, or assuming to act as a broker or salesperson in California must first obtain a DRE license.

findings.explanation: The executive view calls Compass commissions and related expense 81.1% of early-2026 stand-alone brokerage revenue. The cited filing instead reports consolidated 2025 revenue of $6,961.6 million and commissions and related expense of $5,679.7 million, or 81.6%.
findings.materiality: Correcting 81.1% to 81.6% and the period/perimeter description strengthens rather than reverses the thin-company-dollar evidence; the 0.5 percentage-point correction does not plausibly change pi_dist, C, or the low-capture thesis.
findings.remediation: Correct the percentage to 81.6% and describe it as consolidated 2025 Compass revenue and commissions-related expense in any future research packet.
findings.publication_caveat: Compass's filing reports consolidated 2025 commissions and related expense of $5,679.7 million on $6,961.6 million revenue (81.6%), not 81.1% of early-2026 stand-alone brokerage revenue; this correction does not change the low-capture conclusion.

publication_caveats: inputs.ai_replaceable_share: The 3-digit 531 role mix is not representative of exact 531210; maintenance, janitorial, rental and property-management roles largely belong to sibling codes.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: Most real estate agents are independent contractors, so automation of agent tasks is not equivalent to reducing the supplied W-2 wage base or improving brokerage EBITDA.
inputs.pi_dist: Compass and Real are large public models, not a direct measure of every private brokerage; savings from automating 1099 agent work are not operator labor savings.
inputs.pi_moat: A dominant local brand with company-owned leads can be materially stronger; an owner-dependent or agent-portable book can be weaker.
inputs.t50_years: NAR measures individual REALTOR use, not verified brokerage process coverage or labor replacement.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: The survey had a 2.5% response rate and is member-level rather than firm-level; tool use is a broad proxy for effective adoption.
inputs.historical_analogs: Generative AI may unbundle advice and discovery more deeply than prior workflow tools, so the analog may understate terminal pressure.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct: NAR members include sales agents, associate brokers and nonowners; member age cannot be treated as direct owner age.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct.succession_shortage_documented: Firm-level succession planning must be diligenced; member demographics alone do not prove a channel-wide shortage.
inputs.active_consolidators: These are large strategic transactions, not proof of a deep, repeatable buyer pool for independent $1–10M EBITDA brokerages.
inputs.buy_mult: Axial's category may include business mixes beyond exact residential 531210; the single comp is anonymized, and EBITDA must be normalized after owner production and agent splits.

inputs.exit_mult: REMAX includes global franchise and mortgage assets, brand value and $30M expected synergies; it is not a clean independent-brokerage comp, and Axial's observed range is extremely wide.
inputs.pricing_structure: Owned brokerage, franchise, cap, desk-fee, team and ancillary-service models have different revenue bases; compare valuations only on normalized retained EBITDA.

Compass's filing reports consolidated 2025 commissions and related expense of $5,679.7 million on $6,961.6 million revenue (81.6%), not 81.1% of early-2026 stand-alone brokerage revenue; this correction does not change the low-capture conclusion.
summary: Publishable with caveats. Exact-artifact mechanics, dataset injection, arithmetic, finalization, and memo rendering pass. The cited evidence supports the weak-capture brokerage thesis and factor directions; disclosed scope, contractor-labor, ownership, transaction-comparability, and multiple uncertainty remain visible, and one non-material Compass percentage/period label requires a publication caveat.
